artifact.content_preview:
MEETING NOTES — NGIS PHASE 3 STEERING COMMITTEE
Date: March 8, 2026 | Attendees: Director Okafor, Alice Tran, Bob Morrison, ADM Clarke

Item 5 (raised by ADM Clarke):

Following the recent cyber incident affecting a peer department's primary
data centre, ADM Clarke raised the requirement to add a dedicated disaster
recovery (DR) site to the NGIS Phase 3 scope.

ADM Clarke's request:
"Given the current threat environment and Treasury Board's updated guidance
on GC infrastructure resilience, I wou

extracted_tasks.text: Investigate adding a new disaster recovery (DR) site as part of the Phase 3 consolidation, including identifying a colocation facility (Borden or Miramichi) and ensuring hot-standby replicas.
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: I would like the project team to investigate adding a new disaster recovery site as part of the Phase 3 consolidation.
Action: Alice Tran to prepare DR site options analysis. Due: March 15.

extracted_tasks.text: Formal scope addition requires a change request, ADM approval, and Treasury Board notification if cost impact exceeds 15%.
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: Any formal scope addition would require a change request, ADM approval, and Treasury Board notification if the cost impact exceeds 15%.

extracted_tasks.text: Decision: A dedicated disaster recovery (DR) site is essential for business continuity compliance despite being a material scope addition.
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: I believe it is essential for business continuity compliance.

metadata.date: March 8, 2026 | Attendees: Director Okafor, Alice Tran, Bob Morrison, ADM Clarke

```json
{
  "in_scope": [
    "Decommissioning of Sites B and C physical infrastructure",
    "Migration of all server workloads from Sites B and C to Site A",
    "Physical server rack consolidation and cable management at Site A",

    "Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites",
    "Network segmentation reconfiguration per updated security zones",
    "BGP routing table migration and validation",

    "Weekly status reports to SSC Project Management Office",

    "Risk register maintenance",
    "Change management and stakeholder communications"
  ],
  "out_of_scope": [
    "End-user device refresh or replacement (laptops, desktops, mobile devices) — addressed under NGIS Phase 4",
    "Application modernization or re-platforming — no changes to application code, architecture, or hosting platform; lift and shift only",
    "GC-Cloud workload migration to public cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GC-Cloud) — addressed under GC-Cloud Wave 4",
    "End-user training and change management beyond project communications — responsibility of client departments",
    "Procurement of new application licenses — software licensing changes resulting from platform migration",
    "Network monitoring tool upgrades — NOC monitoring platform upgrades deferred to a separate initiative"

    "D-005: User Acceptance Test (UAT) Plan — Due March 10, 2026",
    "D-006: Site A Consolidation Complete (Go-Live) — Due April 30, 2026",
    "D-007: Post-Migration Validation Report — Due May 31, 2026",
    "D-008: Project Closure Report — Due June 30, 2026"
  ],
  "constraints": [
    "Total approved budget is $2,400,000 CAD inclusive of all fees, disbursements, and applicable taxes",
    "Expenditures beyond the approved budget require a formal budget amendment approved by the Director General of IT Infrastructure",
    "Budget variances exceeding 15% require submission to Treasury Board",
    "Go-Live (D-006) must be achieved no later than April 30, 2026",
    "Final project closure date is June 30, 2026 (end of fiscal year 2025–2026)",
    "Extensions beyond June 30, 2026 require ADM-level approval",
    "All work must comply with ITSG-33: IT Security Risk Management — A Lifecycle Approach",
    "All work must comply with the Government of Canada Cloud Adoption Strategy",
    "All work must comply with the Treasury Board Policy on Government Security",
    "All work must comply with the Privacy Act (R.S.C., 1985, c. P-21)",
    "All client-facing deliverables must comply with the Official Languages Act",
    "Scope, schedule, or budget changes must follow the SSC Project Change Management Process",

  ]
}
```

AD schema extension and forest restructuring
Group Policy Object (GPO) consolidation and testing
Integration testing with downstream applications dependent on AD
Weekly status reports to SSC Project Management Office
out_of_scope: End-user device refresh or replacement (laptops, desktops, mobile devices) — addressed under NGIS Phase 4
Application modernization or re-platforming — no changes to application code, architecture, or hosting platform; lift and shift only
GC-Cloud workload migration to public cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GC-Cloud) — addressed under GC-Cloud Wave 4
End-user training and change management beyond project communications — responsibility of client departments
Procurement of new application licenses — software licensing changes resulting from platform migration
Network monitoring tool upgrades — NOC monitoring platform upgrades deferred to a separate initiative
deliverables: D-001: Project Management Plan — Due October 31, 2024
D-002: Technical Design Document (TDD) — Due December 15, 2024
D-003: Security Assessment and Authorization (SA&A) Package — Due January 31, 2025
D-004: Migration Runbook (Sites B and C) — Due February 28, 2025

constraints: Total approved budget is $2,400,000 CAD inclusive of all fees, disbursements, and applicable taxes

llm_calls.content:
```json
{
  "alignment_score": 0.05,
  "drift_type": "scope_expansion",
  "detected_ask": "ADM Clarke's request to add a dedicated disaster recovery (DR) site — including identification of a colocation facility (Borden or Miramichi) and hot-standby workload replication for all Phase 3 migrated workloads — as part of the NGIS Phase 3 consolidation scope.",
  "baseline_reference": "IN SCOPE: Migration of all server workloads from Sites B and C to Site A (consolidation to a single Site A target only; no secondary or DR site identified); DELIVERABLES: D-006 Site A Consolidation Complete (Go-Live) — April 30, 2026 (single-site go-live; no DR go-live deliverable exists); CONSTRAINTS: Total approved budget $2,400,000 CAD — a hot-standby DR site would represent a material cost addition likely exceeding the 15% Treasury Board variance threshold; Go-Live no later than April 30, 2026 — DR site buildout would introduce schedule risk to this hard constraint.",
  "suggested_action": "The Project Manager (Alice Tran) has correctly identified this as a formal scope addition requiring a structured change control response. The PM should: (1) Complete the DR site options analysis by March 15 as committed, including at minimum three options — proceed with DR in Phase 3, defer to a future phase, and a hybrid partial-DR approach; (2) Quantify cost, schedule, and resource impacts for each option against the approved $2,400,000 CAD budget and April 30, 2026 Go-Live constraint; (3) Formally log a Change Request (CR) against SOW-NGIS-P3-2024-001 regardless of which option is selected; (4) Obtain ADM Clarke's written approval as the requesting authority before any scope work begins; (5) If cost impact exceeds 15% of approved budget (~$360,000 CAD threshold), prepare a Treasury Board submission per the budget variance constraint; (6) Assess whether the April 30, 2026 Go-Live date remains achievable under any DR-inclusive scenario and escalate a schedule change request to ADM level if an extension beyond June 30, 2026 is required; (7) Update the Technical Design Document (D-002), Migration Runbook (D-004), and SA&A Package (D-003) if the CR is approved, as all three would be materially affected by the addition of a DR site.",
  "reasoning": "This document represents a clear and significant scope expansion. The entire NGIS Phase 3 scope baseline is architected around a single-destination consolidation — decommissioning Sites B and C and migrating all workloads to Site A. There is no reference anywhere in the baseline to a secondary site, disaster recovery infrastructure, colocation facilities, or hot-standby replication. The addition of a DR site would introduce a new physical location, new network topology requirements, new environmental systems considerations, new BGP routing complexity, new AD replication topology, new security assessment obligations under ITSG-33, and substantial new procurement and cost exposure — all of which fall outside the current SOW. ADM Clarke himself explicitly acknowledged this is not in the current SOW and constitutes a material scope addition. The alignment score of 0.05 reflects that the document is almost entirely out of scope, with the only in-scope element being Alice Tran's procedurally correct response invoking the change request and Treasury Board notification process already defined in the project constraints. No unauthorized work has been initiated, which is the sole factor preventing a score of 0.0."
}
```

parsed_json.detected_ask: ADM Clarke's request to add a dedicated disaster recovery (DR) site — including identification of a colocation facility (Borden or Miramichi) and hot-standby workload replication for all Phase 3 migrated workloads — as part of the NGIS Phase 3 consolidation scope.
parsed_json.baseline_reference: IN SCOPE: Migration of all server workloads from Sites B and C to Site A (consolidation to a single Site A target only; no secondary or DR site identified); DELIVERABLES: D-006 Site A Consolidation Complete (Go-Live) — April 30, 2026 (single-site go-live; no DR go-live deliverable exists); CONSTRAINTS: Total approved budget $2,400,000 CAD — a hot-standby DR site would represent a material cost addition likely exceeding the 15% Treasury Board variance threshold; Go-Live no later than April 30, 2026 — DR site buildout would introduce schedule risk to this hard constraint.
parsed_json.suggested_action: The Project Manager (Alice Tran) has correctly identified this as a formal scope addition requiring a structured change control response. The PM should: (1) Complete the DR site options analysis by March 15 as committed, including at minimum three options — proceed with DR in Phase 3, defer to a future phase, and a hybrid partial-DR approach; (2) Quantify cost, schedule, and resource impacts for each option against the approved $2,400,000 CAD budget and April 30, 2026 Go-Live constraint; (3) Formally log a Change Request (CR) against SOW-NGIS-P3-2024-001 regardless of which option is selected; (4) Obtain ADM Clarke's written approval as the requesting authority before any scope work begins; (5) If cost impact exceeds 15% of approved budget (~$360,000 CAD threshold), prepare a Treasury Board submission per the budget variance constraint; (6) Assess whether the April 30, 2026 Go-Live date remains achievable under any DR-inclusive scenario and escalate a schedule change request to ADM level if an extension beyond June 30, 2026 is required; (7) Update the Technical Design Document (D-002), Migration Runbook (D-004), and SA&A Package (D-003) if the CR is approved, as all three would be materially affected by the addition of a DR site.
parsed_json.reasoning: This document represents a clear and significant scope expansion. The entire NGIS Phase 3 scope baseline is architected around a single-destination consolidation — decommissioning Sites B and C and migrating all workloads to Site A. There is no reference anywhere in the baseline to a secondary site, disaster recovery infrastructure, colocation facilities, or hot-standby replication. The addition of a DR site would introduce a new physical location, new network topology requirements, new environmental systems considerations, new BGP routing complexity, new AD replication topology, new security assessment obligations under ITSG-33, and substantial new procurement and cost exposure — all of which fall outside the current SOW. ADM Clarke himself explicitly acknowledged this is not in the current SOW and constitutes a material scope addition. The alignment score of 0.05 reflects that the document is almost entirely out of scope, with the only in-scope element being Alice Tran's procedurally correct response invoking the change request and Treasury Board notification process already defined in the project constraints. No unauthorized work has been initiated, which is the sole factor preventing a score of 0.0.
